2b1ab06299308040a986fb71a7b5dcfb.title: Moravian - Executive Rec Memo (EAB) (1)
2b1ab06299308040a986fb71a7b5dcfb.characteristics:
Email Lee sent to SLT (Bryon, Dee, Scott)

Bryon, Dee, and Scott: We wrapped up the full assessment of EAB in relation to SPSI marketing and enrollment operations, and we now have a clear, evidence-based plan to move forward. The checkpoint model we outline in the attached memo gives Moravian the structure, pace, and accountability needed to keep a close eye on EAB alignment while protecting SPSI ability to act quickly if performance doesn improve. We will work alongside your marketing, enrollment, and SPSI leads to stand up the checkpoints, finalize the financial and pacing model, and ensure you have full visibility into funnel performance as we move toward the Spring and Fall 2025 cycles. We will follow up separately to schedule the kickoff for the checkpoint implementation. Also, Bryon, feel free to stop at the executive summary in the PDF, it should be enough for your level. Should you want to dig deeper, by all means go for it! Thank you. *Lee Bradshaw**

2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 1  - Due 11/30/25
2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.characteristics:
By Nov 30, EAB must agree to the FY24-25 SPSI budget projections, volume projections, and cost-per-student model that Rhodes Advisors and SPSI leadership are finalizing. This will serve as the financial baseline for measuring performance and pacing.
- All SPSI programs fully reactivated across paid and organic journeys.
- Initial improvements to attribution and source tracking.

2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.title: Moravian - Post February Oversight - Ongoing
2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.characteristics: ## **Post-February Oversight** **Weekly Performance Reviews:** Rhodes Advisors and Moravian will jointly review SPSI performance every week, with a focus on lead volume, CPL, conversion quality, and pacing toward Fall 2025 goals. **Financial Stewardship:** All performance evaluations will be measured against the SPSI budget, volume projections, and cost-per-student model that EAB has agreed to and be shared with Moravian leadership. **Pipeline Health Monitoring:** SPSI must demonstrate consistent inquiry growth, improved lead quality, and stable movement through the funnel. **Fail-Safe Protocol:** If weekly progress stalls or vendor coordination breaks down Moravian will escalate toward transition planning. Vendor contracts for alternative solutions will be drafted in parallel to avoid delays. **Clear Ownership Model:** SPSI oversight of EAB will be centralized. We recommend that Justine Bartholomew (institutional lead) and Rhodes Advisors (strategic/performance lead) jointly own EAB coordination, KPI management, and integration across enrollment, brand, and program teams.

330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.title: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment -Packet
330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.characteristics:
Project: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment
Objective Produce a concise, data-backed go/no-go recommendation on the MS in Cybersecurity program by evaluating market demand, enrollment feasibility, and competitive positioning in Moravian's target geography.
Primary Research Questions
Is there sufficient, quantifiable demand for a master's-level cybersecurity program in the Greater Bethlehem / Eastern Pennsylvania region?
Is the program's competitive environment navigable is there a realistic, defensible path to enrollment growth?
Is current enrollment underperformance more likely attributable to a market demand problem or an execution and positioning problem?
Based on the evidence, what is the recommended path forward: continue, continue with defined changes, or discontinue?

Scope of Work
Demand Analysis Quantify current and near-term employer demand for cybersecurity talent in the target region using labor market data. Assess degree-level requirements versus alternative credentials (bootcamps, certifications) among employers and prospective students. Identify the size and accessibility of the addressable student population.

Enrollment Feasibility Modeling Using available market data and RA benchmarks, develop a directional enrollment model estimating realistic cohort potential. Identify the primary friction points demand volume, price sensitivity, modality, competitive alternatives that most directly constrain enrollment performance.

Competitive Landscape Assessment Evaluate 5-8 directly competing master's-level cybersecurity programs on dimensions including enrollment model, pricing, modality, target audience, and positioning. Assess degree of market saturation and identify whether meaningful differentiation is achievable.

Performance Gap Diagnosis Using market data and available program performance indicators, assess whether current enrollment gaps are more consistent with a structural demand problem or a correctable execution and positioning problem. This distinction is central to the go/no-go recommendation.

Go / No-Go Recommendation Synthesize findings into a clear, evidence-based
recommendation:
Go market conditions support continued investment
Go with Conditions viable with specific, defined changes to audience, positioning, or program structure
No-Go market evidence does not support continued investment at this time Each recommendation will be supported by the quantitative and qualitative evidence gathered across the scope above.

Deliverable
A concise written analysis report including:
Demand landscape summary with supporting data
Enrollment feasibility assessment with directional modeling
Competitive landscape summary
Performance gap diagnosis
Go / No-Go recommendation with supporting rationale
If Go with Conditions: specific, actionable conditions defined

The format will be a tight written memo or brief deck whichever Micah determines best serves clarity and speed given the timeline. Completeness of evidence takes priority over format.

33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.title: 30Credit MBA Financial Analysis
33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.characteristics: Rhodes Advisors was engaged to evaluate Moravian University's proposed restructuring of its MBA program from 36 to 30 credits, with a specific focus on assessing the financial sustainability and market viability of revised tuition price points at $21,000, $25,000, and $30,000. The goal of this analysis is to quantify the revenue and enrollment implications of the credit reduction, determine the enrollment thresholds required to offset per-student revenue loss, and assess whether each pricing scenario is defensible within the competitive market ultimately informing a data-driven recommendation on optimal program pricing and positioning.

351ab0629930808194a2ece92b60e2d6.title: MBA Aligned Certficate Recommendation Memo
351ab0629930808194a2ece92b60e2d6.characteristics: **1. MBA-Aligned Certificate Recommendation Memo** A strategic recommendation outlining the strongest certificate pathways to support the 30-credit MBA, including priority focus areas (starting with AI-aligned offerings), market rationale, positioning, and how certificates should function as stackable pathways into the degree. **2. Project Plan + Go / No-Go Framework** A practical execution plan that defines timeline, ownership, operational requirements, and decision points needed to determine whether Moravian can realistically launch and impact Fall 2026 enrollment.

351ab062993080868d02c2e9995f51d4.title: Leadership Review & Decision
351ab062993080868d02c2e9995f51d4.characteristics: **1. Leadership Review + Decision Support** A presentation and discussion framework for Dee, Amy, Bryon, and academic leadership that supports alignment, decision-making, and next-step planning around certificate launch priorities and broader MBA portfolio strategy.

356ab06299308094b79ac42a1c91f49d.title: Add set Deposit field to Automate Admit notification and admittee response for SPSI
356ab06299308094b79ac42a1c91f49d.characteristics: The FormAssembly Acceptance Agreement connector has been updated to include the requested changes to the deposit fields. It will now update Salesforce when a prospective student selects **"Accept"** on the Acceptance Agreement, it will not update deposit fields for deferrals or declines. **Student Stage Update** API Name = Student_Stage_Update__c Value = Deposit Paid **Deposit Paid Date** API Name = TargetX_SRMb__Deposit_Paid_Date__c Value = Same date as form completion date **Deposit Amount** API Name = TargetX_SRMb__Deposit_Amount__c Value = 0
